sourceMovie.title: Sleepy Hollow
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sleepy Hollow (1999) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Pale Blue Eye
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sleepy Hollow clicked for you, The Pale Blue Eye (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sleepy Hollow?
faq.answer: Try The Pale Blue Eye, The Car, It—each shares DNA with Sleepy Hollow while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sleepy Hollow?
faq.answer: Sleepy Hollow (1999) is best described as Fantasy, Thriller, Mystery, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sleepy Hollow worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
